title: Nami’s Scheme in Orange Town
description: On pages 4–6, this part develops the danger of Buggy’s crew and the unstable alliance between Luffy, Zoro, and Nami.
scene: Orange Town becomes the stage for Nami’s schemes, Buggy’s violence, and Luffy’s refusal to act like a normal pirate.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It is the GoldenAge of Pinacy. Countless pirates sail the seas, searching for leg- endary pirate Gold Roger's mysterious treasure,the "One Piece." Among them is Monkey D. Luffy, who grew up listen-ing to the wild tales of buccaneer "Red- "

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: He ate the fruit of the Gum-Gum Tree, gaining stretchy powers.He wants to become King of the Pirates—and find his hero, "Red- Haired" Sharks.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Although he's won fame as a pirate hunter, his true dream is to become the world's greatest swordsman.

summary: The Orange Town conflict mixes Nami’s suspicious cleverness with Buggy’s theatrical cruelty. The section builds the unstable alliance that will oppose Buggy’s crew.
